mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings pressure, triggering immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or signal loses predictive power with p-value > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and generating negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >60% of observations, or regime-conditional IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with slowing payroll employment growth creates stagflationary pressure that forces equity risk premiums higher, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between FEDFUNDS and PAYEMS yields a positive or null coefficient in multivariate regression, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >58% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annualized signals a contractionary demand environment that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the consumer price index above 315 reflects persistent inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing long-term equity valuations.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 315 are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in >60% of months, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate coinciding with falling industrial production confirms a broad-based economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings expectations and generates negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration in UNRATE and INDPRO fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, with positive outcomes occurring in >55% of cases or interaction term p-value > 0.05.
